reasoning: Empty set for `lo == hi` is the correct and expected behavior of a half-open range `[lo, hi)`. This is a basic property of the definition.
A half-open range `[lo, hi)` containing `lo` when `lo < hi` is the standard expected behavior. This follows directly from the set comprehension definition.
Excluding `hi` from `[lo, hi)` is the defining property of a half-open range. This is correct by design.
A range of width 1 having exactly one element is a correct consequence of the verified `lemma_nat_range` which proves `len == hi - lo`.
Subset monotonicity when widening the lower bound is a correct set-theoretic property. `[b, c) ⊆ [a, c)` when `a <= b` follows directly from the membership predicate.
